rules.section: Shared Utils — escHtml, debounce, and Common Utilities
rules.summary: escHtml must escape all 5 HTML entities

rules.section: Draft Auto-Save Pattern for Field Forms
rules.summary: Draft storage keys must be worker_name-scoped

rules.section: Shared Utils — escHtml, debounce, and Common Utilities
rules.summary: Shared utilities must be loaded from utils.js
rules.rationale: Duplicated utility functions cause inconsistent behavior and security issues

rules.summary: debounce function must use setTimeout/clearTimeout

rules.section: Transient UI State Must Be Persisted to Be Editable
rules.summary: Edit modals must pre-populate stored parts_used

rules.rationale: Silent feature breakage when new form options are added without updating the map

rules.section: New Page Deployment — Two Registries Must Be Updated Together
rules.summary: Update nav-hub and index.html stageData in the same commit
rules.pattern: nav-hub entry included in the same PR as the page itself
rules.rationale: Invisible pages to new users when nav-hub and stageData are out of sync

rules.section: New Page Spec Must Name the Exact Identity Key
rules.summary: Specify the exact localStorage key to read in new page specs
rules.pattern: exact localStorage key to read specified in the auth section
rules.rationale: Silent redirects with no error when wrong key is used

rules.section: WorkerFilter Pattern — Only Apply to Tables That Have the Column
rules.summary: Only apply workerFilter to tables with both hive_id and worker_name columns
rules.pattern: table has both hive_id and worker_name columns
rules.rationale: Silent Supabase errors when workerFilter is applied to tables missing one of the columns

rules.summary: Document access pattern in schema comment for child tables
rules.pattern: access pattern documented in schema comment
rules.rationale: Future queries don't guess access pattern

rules.section: Foreign Key Column Type Must Match Exactly — uuid vs text
rules.summary: hive_id columns must be declared as uuid, not text
rules.rationale: Supabase will reject FK constraint if hive_id is text while referenced id is uuid (error 42804).

rules.section: Foreign Key Column Type Must Match Exactly — uuid vs text
rules.summary: Columns referencing uuid id must not be typed as text
rules.rationale: Type mismatch causes 42804 error; referencing column must match target uuid type.

rules.summary: pm_completion_id column must be uuid type
rules.rationale: FK column pm_completion_id should be uuid to match pm_completions.id.

rules.section: Prevent Duplicates at Entry, Not at Display
rules.summary: JS insertion code should check for existing item case‑insensitively
rules.rationale: Prevent duplicate records by querying for existing natural key before insert.

rules.section: Smart Collapse for Long Lists — Pre-Compute Status at Fetch Time
rules.summary: Item objects should have a .status property assigned during fetch
rules.rationale: Pre‑computing status at fetch avoids recomputation on each render.

rules.summary: Use Layer 1 or 2 for automation
rules.rationale: Reach for Layer 1 or 2 first — they are already inside Supabase, require no additional infrastructure, and are free.

rules.summary: Keep SQL minimal
rules.rationale: pg_cron jobs call Edge Functions via HTTP — the Edge Function contains all the logic. The SQL is just the trigger.

rules.section: DB Webhook — Instant Reaction to Data Changes
rules.summary: DB Webhooks are stateless
rules.pattern: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget
rules.rationale: If the Edge Function fails, there is no retry by default. For critical alerts (safety, compliance), add a `webhook_log` table and write a row on every trigger so failures are visible.

rules.summary: Use Resend for email
rules.pattern: Resend is the email provider of choice
rules.rationale: Resend has the most generous free tier, clean API, and a Supabase Edge Function integration in one HTTP call.

rules.summary: Store inputs, results, and narrative separately
rules.pattern: inputs       jsonb,      -- all form inputs as submitted
rules.rationale: Store inputs, results, AND narrative separately in JSONB. This allows re-rendering the report from history without re-calling the AI.

rules.section: Feature Support Maps — Key by All Relevant Dimensions, Not Just One
rules.summary: BOM_SOW_SUPPORTED must be an object, not a flat array.
rules.rationale: A flat list only works when there is exactly one category — the moment a second category is added, the flat list breaks or requires a full restructure.

rules.section: Mandatory Minimums Are Edge Function Constants, Not Client Defaults
rules.summary: Edge Function must define PPC_MIN_TANK_L constant.
rules.rationale: Edge Function constants cannot be bypassed from the client; they enforce mandatory minimums.

rules.section: Mandatory Minimums Are Edge Function Constants, Not Client Defaults
rules.summary: Edge Function must enforce minimum using Math.max.
rules.rationale: If the Edge Function doesn't enforce the minimum, the report can silently under-size a safety-critical system.

rules.section: Hive Gate Scope — Only Gate Pages With Hive-Scoped Data
rules.summary: Standalone tool pages should not contain hard hive gate.
rules.rationale: Hard hive gate blocks solo users silently; standalone tools must not be gated.

rules.section: Flow Check Formulas — Verify the Pass/Fail Ratio Is Not Constant
rules.summary: Check if design flow and minimum flow are not constant
rules.rationale: Verify that the check is actually sensitive to user input before shipping

rules.section: Engineering Calc — Impedance Must Use Complex Magnitude, Not Scalar Sum
rules.summary: Use complex magnitude for impedance calculation
rules.rationale: Cable impedance must be calculated using complex magnitude

rules.section: Engineering Calc — Location-Keyed Lookup Tables for Per-City Defaults
rules.summary: Use location-keyed lookup tables for per-city defaults
rules.rationale: Keep city-specific engineering constants versioned in code

rules.section: Model-Agnostic Architecture — Build Once, Swap Models
rules.summary: Swap models with a config change, not a rewrite
rules.rationale: Swap models with a config change, not a rewrite

rules.summary: Use function calling to decide which agents to invoke
rules.rationale: Decide which agents to invoke using function calling

rules.summary: Use Promise.allSettled, not Promise.all
rules.rationale: Other agents must still return results if one fails

rules.section: Supabase Edge Function — Keep API Key Server-Side
rules.summary: API keys must not appear in client HTML files.
rules.rationale: Never put the Claude or Gemini API key in client HTML.

rules.section: Supabase Edge Function — Keep API Key Server-Side
rules.summary: Edge functions must read API keys from Deno.env.
rules.rationale: API key is in Deno.env — never sent to client

rules.section: Supabase Edge Function — Keep API Key Server-Side
rules.summary: Edge function should import serve from Deno standard HTTP server.
rules.rationale: import { serve } from 'https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts';

rules.section: Sentinel Agent Pattern — May 2026 Hardening Loop
rules.summary: sentinelCheckPermissions must be called before orchestrate's main logic.
rules.rationale: await sentinelCheckPermissions(...); const agentsToRun = await modelClient.planAgents...

rules.section: Sentinel Agent Pattern — May 2026 Hardening Loop
rules.summary: sentinelCheckPermissions must validate presence of hiveRole.
rules.rationale: if (!hiveRole) { throw new Error('No HIVE_ROLE in context …'); }

rules.section: Sentinel Agent Pattern — May 2026 Hardening Loop
rules.summary: sentinelCheckPermissions should return true on successful validation.
rules.rationale: return true; // context is valid

rules.section: Gemini Function Calling Pattern (Free Tier Orchestrator)
rules.summary: Gemini calls must use tool_config with mode AUTO.
rules.rationale: tool_config: { function_calling_config: { mode: 'AUTO' } }

rules.section: Gemini Function Calling Pattern (Free Tier Orchestrator)
rules.summary: Gemini function calling should declare functionDeclarations array.
rules.rationale: functionDeclarations: [ { name: 'run_failure_analysis', ... } ]

rules.section: Groq API — Reliability Rules for BOM/SOW and Long-Output Agents
rules.summary: Set max_tokens explicitly on every Groq call that produces structured documents.
rules.rationale: Groq's default output token limit is too low for agents that generate large structured documents.

rules.section: Fallback Chain — Order by TPM Capacity, Not Model Quality
rules.summary: Put openai/gpt-oss-* models last in any fallback chain.
rules.rationale: Their 8K TPM cap means large prompts will 413 immediately.

rules.section: Treat HTTP 413 as Skip, Not Fatal Error
rules.summary: Treat 413 from Groq as skip, not fatal error.
rules.rationale: A 413 from Groq means 'prompt too large for this model's TPM tier' — not a code bug.

rules.section: No Em Dashes in Prompt Text
rules.summary: Never use em dashes in any LLM prompt template.
rules.rationale: Em dashes get copied verbatim by the model into its output JSON.

rules.summary: Select only columns the agent needs.
rules.rationale: Agents get expensive fast if data payloads are bloated.

rules.summary: Summarize rows before passing to model.
rules.rationale: Join fields into one-line strings, not full JSON objects.

rules.summary: Cap row count at 200.
rules.rationale: Never pass more than 200 rows to a single agent call.

rules.summary: Output structured JSON, not prose.
rules.rationale: JSON is shorter and parseable by the orchestrator without another model call.

rules.summary: Keep agent instructions in a const at the top of the file.
rules.rationale: This enables prompt caching when you upgrade to paid.

rules.summary: Every agent query must include .eq('hive_id', hiveId).
rules.rationale: Agents must be hive-scoped — never return data from another hive.

rules.section: AI API Abuse Protection — Rate Limiting (MANDATORY Before Shipping)
rules.summary: Edge Function must call checkAIRateLimit before any model call
rules.rationale: The rate limit check must be the first operation in every Edge Function to prevent abuse.

rules.section: AI API Abuse Protection — Rate Limiting (MANDATORY Before Shipping)
rules.summary: Edge Function response must include remaining AI calls count
rules.rationale: The client UI can display remaining calls per hour.

rules.summary: JS math must be deterministic
rules.rationale: LLMs hallucinate numbers, JS is deterministic

rules.section: Gemini Flash Free Tier — Default for Non-Critical Narrative
rules.summary: Use Gemini Flash for report narrative generation
rules.rationale: sufficient for professional prose and costs nothing

rules.section: Edge Function Routing by calc_type — 4 Sync Points
rules.summary: Update all 4 sync points when adding a new calc type
rules.rationale: silent break if not updated

rules.section: Deno Edge Function — Never Use Object Shorthand With Mismatched Variable Names
rules.summary: Use explicit key:value syntax in Deno Edge Function return objects
rules.rationale: silent runtime error if not used

rules.section: Debugging Edge Function 500 Errors — Use curl, Not the Frontend SDK
rules.summary: Use curl to diagnose Edge Function 500 errors
rules.rationale: actual error body is never exposed to the browser

rules.summary: Follow the checklist when adding a new calculation type
rules.rationale: silent break if not updated

rules.section: Gemini API Key Types — GCP Console vs AI Studio (Free Tier)
rules.summary: Verify accessible models before building the main agent
rules.rationale: silent break if not updated

rules.summary: Use GROQ_API_KEY in Authorization header
rules.rationale: Forces clean JSON — no markdown fences to strip.

rules.section: BOM+SOW Agent Prompts — Conditional Content Produces Better Documents
rules.summary: Pass conditional context into the prompt
rules.rationale: Produces significantly more accurate and actionable output.

rules.section: BOM+SOW Agent Prompts — Conditional Content Produces Better Documents
rules.summary: Use load type flag in prompt
rules.rationale: Produces different BOM items and SOW clauses.

rules.section: BOM+SOW Agent Prompts — Conditional Content Produces Better Documents
rules.summary: Use space type in prompt
rules.rationale: Selects correct CCT without engineer specifying it.

rules.summary: Use SOW agent schema with content: string
rules.rationale: Returns sections in exact shape.

rules.summary: Use defensive renderer pattern
rules.rationale: Handles legacy/malformed responses.

rules.section: Supabase Secrets — Redeploy Required After Update
rules.summary: Redeploy after updating Supabase secret
rules.rationale: Picks up new value without silent no-op.

rules.summary: SOW section schema always uses `content` — one full paragraph per section
rules.pattern: sow_sections: array of 7 sections \(each: section_no, title, content\)
rules.rationale: The model echoes back the format and length you describe in the prompt.

rules.summary: The prompt must include full sentence starters ("The Contractor shall supply...")
rules.rationale: The model echoes back the format and length you describe in the prompt.

rules.section: Narrative Fallback — Add for Every New Calc Type on Build Day
rules.summary: The fallback must reference specific result fields (`rec.selected_kvar`, `rec.current_reduction_pct`, etc.)
rules.rationale: New calc types with no entry fall through to the generic "Refer to the results summary table" message — unhelpful and looks broken.

rules.section: BOM/SOW Agent Functions — Always Use `callGroq()`, Never Direct Fetch
rules.summary: Every BOM/SOW agent function must use the local `callGroq(prompt)` wrapper
rules.rationale: This routes through the full 15-model multi-provider chain with the 8000 token ceiling BOM/SOW needs.

rules.section: Multi-Provider AI Chain — Shared Module Pattern (Current Architecture)
rules.summary: All LLM calls across every edge function go through a single shared module: `supabase/functions/_shared/ai-chain.ts`
rules.rationale: No function embeds its own chain or raw `fetch()` to any provider.

rules.section: Multi-Provider AI Chain — Shared Module Pattern (Current Architecture)

rules.section: Treat HTTP 413 as Skip, Not Fatal Error

rules.rationale: A 413 from Groq means 'prompt too large for this model's TPM tier' — not a code bug.

rules.section: No Em Dashes in Prompt Text
rules.summary: Never use em dashes in any LLM prompt template.
rules.rationale: Em dashes get copied verbatim by the model into its output JSON.

rules.summary: Select only columns the agent needs.

rules.summary: Summarize rows before passing to model.

rules.summary: Cap row count at 200.
rules.rationale: Never pass more than 200 rows to a single agent call.

rules.summary: Output structured JSON, not prose.

rules.summary: Keep agent instructions in a const at the top of the file.

rules.summary: Every agent query must include .eq('hive_id', hiveId).

rules.section: Diagnosing Edge Function 500 Errors — curl Is the Only Reliable Method
rules.summary: Diagnose Edge Function 500 errors with curl
rules.pattern: curl -s -X POST \s+"https://YOUR_PROJECT\.supabase\.co/functions/v1/FUNCTION_NAME" \s+-H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANON_KEY" \s+-H "Content-Type: application/json" \s+-d '{.*?}' | cat
rules.rationale: The Supabase JS SDK hides the actual error body and only surfaces a non-2xx status code.

rules.section: API Validation After Every Edge Function Deploy

rules.rationale: The browser masks the real response body; curl exposes it.

rules.summary: No render-blocking scripts in <head>
rules.rationale: Render-blocking scripts can slow down page load.

rules.summary: Images have explicit width and height
rules.rationale: Explicit image dimensions prevent layout shift (CLS).

rules.summary: Supabase queries use .select('specific,columns')
rules.rationale: Optimized Supabase queries reduce load time.

rules.summary: No N+1 query patterns
rules.rationale: N+1 query patterns slow down load time.

rules.summary: Animations use only transform and opacity
rules.rationale: GPU-composited animations improve performance.

rules.summary: Floating AI widget script is deferred
rules.rationale: Deferred scripts improve page load time.

rules.summary: No synchronous localStorage reads blocking render
rules.rationale: Synchronous localStorage reads slow down load time.

rules.summary: Parallel async init calls use Promise.all()
rules.rationale: Parallel async init calls improve load time.

rules.section: 0. CRITICAL: Moving supabase-js to end of body breaks pages that call supabase.createClient() at top level
rules.summary: Supabase JS must be loaded at the end of the body
rules.rationale: Moving supabase-js to end of body breaks pages that call supabase.createClient() at top level

rules.section: 1. Check WHERE CDN scripts are loaded, not just whether they're deferred
rules.summary: Supabase JS must not be loaded in the head
rules.rationale: Supabase JS must NOT be in <head> — move it to the end of <body>

rules.section: 1. Check WHERE CDN scripts are loaded, not just whether they're deferred
rules.summary: Tailwind CDN must be loaded in the head
rules.rationale: Tailwind CDN must stay in <head> — it scans the DOM, so it cannot be safely deferred without causing a flash of unstyled content

rules.section: 2. Flag sequential async init calls — always check the bottom of the script block
rules.summary: Sequential async init calls must be replaced with Promise.all
rules.rationale: Sequential async init calls add up fast on 200ms+ latency

rules.section: 3. `filter` animations require `will-change: filter` — not just `will-change: transform`
rules.summary: will-change: filter must be set for filter animations
rules.rationale: Filter changes trigger repaint on every frame and cannot be GPU-composited unless will-change: filter is explicitly set

rules.section: 4. Cache the entry being edited — do not re-fetch from DB in the save handler
rules.summary: Cache the entry being edited in a module-level variable
rules.rationale: Calling loadEntries() inside saveEdit() to retrieve the existing row adds a full Supabase round-trip on every save

rules.section: 5. All Board Data Loads Must Be in Promise.allSettled — Including New Feature Panels
rules.summary: All board data loads must be in Promise.allSettled
rules.rationale: Promise.allSettled lets the rest of the board render normally if one load fails

rules.section: In-Memory Cache + reload Flag Pattern — Avoid Re-Fetching on Filter
rules.summary: Function should accept a reload parameter defaulting to true
rules.rationale: Ensures the function can control data fetching via a reload flag to avoid unnecessary network requests

rules.rationale: Ensures accurate compliance metrics

rules.rationale: Prevents overstatement of compliance

rules.rationale: Prevents silent omission of fields

rules.section: `hive_id` Must Be Included on Edits, Not Just Inserts
rules.summary: Include hive ID in edit payloads
rules.rationale: Ensures accurate hive scoping

rules.summary: Await write results instead of using fire-and-forget
rules.pattern: db\.from\('inventory_items'\).update\({ qty_on_hand: newQty \}\).eq\('id', id\)\.then\(({ error }) => { if \(error\) console\.error\(error\); });
rules.rationale: Ensures write results are visible to the user

rules.section: User-Entered Fields Take Priority Over Computed Values
rules.summary: Prefer user-entered fields over computed values

rules.rationale: Ensures accurate and reliable metrics

rules.section: Metric Queries Must Filter to the Metric's Definition
rules.summary: Filter metric queries to the metric's definition
rules.rationale: Ensures accurate and reliable metrics

rules.section: renderEntries(reload) — Flag Pattern to Avoid Re-Fetching on Filter
rules.summary: Event listeners must call renderEntries(false) to avoid re-fetching data
rules.rationale: Filter/search/sort event listeners must call renderEntries(false). Only initial load, manual refresh, and post-save calls use renderEntries(true).

rules.summary: Cross-page inserts into logbook must include 'category' column
rules.rationale: Missing columns in cross-page inserts can break filters, sorts, and displays on the target page.

rules.summary: Cross-page inserts into logbook must include 'closed_at' column
rules.rationale: Missing 'closed_at' when status is 'Closed' breaks page B's filters and sorts.

rules.summary: Cross-page inserts into logbook must include 'maintenance_type' column
rules.rationale: Missing 'maintenance_type' breaks page B's display and filters.

rules.summary: Cross-page inserts into pm_completions must include 'hive_id' column
rules.rationale: Missing 'hive_id' breaks hive-aware page B's filters and sorts.

rules.summary: Cross-page inserts into pm_completions must include 'worker_name' column
rules.rationale: Missing 'worker_name' breaks page B's worker-based filters.

rules.summary: Cross-page inserts into checklist_records must include 'hive_id' column
rules.rationale: Missing 'hive_id' breaks hive-aware page B's filters.

rules.section: Category Mapping — Use Task Context, Not Parent Entity
rules.summary: Do not use parent entity's category for task mapping
rules.rationale: Using the parent entity's category can lead to incorrect mappings when tasks belong to different categories than their parent entities.

rules.section: Index Every Filter Column at Table Creation Time
rules.summary: Table creation SQL must include a composite index on (hive_id, worker_name) in same file
rules.rationale: Indexes must be added in the same SQL block as CREATE TABLE to avoid later performance issues.

rules.section: Index Every Filter Column at Table Creation Time
rules.summary: SQL must create an index on created_at DESC for new tables
rules.rationale: Primary sort column needs an index to keep list queries fast.

rules.section: List Queries: Narrow Selects + Keyset Pagination
rules.summary: List queries must use .limit(50) and not other limits
rules.rationale: Enforce .limit(50) on all list queries to prevent unbounded fetches.

rules.section: List Queries: Narrow Selects + Keyset Pagination
rules.summary: List view queries must not use select('*')
rules.rationale: Narrow selects avoid fetching large JSON/blobs and improve performance.

rules.section: Realtime Subscriptions — Always Filter by hive_id
rules.summary: Realtime subscription calls must include a filter property
rules.rationale: Filtering by hive_id prevents high-frequency noise from unrelated hives.

rules.section: Analytics: Postgres Views and RPC Functions
rules.summary: Do not use .reduce() on full-table fetches; use views or RPCs instead
rules.rationale: Aggregations over >200 rows should be done in Postgres, not client-side JS.

rules.summary: Schedule SQL to run on a schedule, calling an Edge Function
rules.rationale: pg_cron runs SQL on a schedule — the SQL calls an Edge Function that does the actual work.

rules.summary: Pre-compute aggregations and store in a report table
rules.rationale: Any data that requires aggregating across many rows or running an AI agent must be pre-computed by a scheduled job and stored in a report table.

rules.summary: Use DB Webhook for instant reactions to single row changes
rules.rationale: Use DB Webhook when You need instant reaction to one row change.

rules.summary: Use n8n for multi-step time-based sequences
rules.pattern: hive_members INSERT \(new worker joined\) \-\> DB Webhook \-\> n8n
rules.rationale: n8n handles orchestration of multi-step time-based sequences.

rules.section: Webhook Log Table — Make Failures Visible
rules.summary: Write to automation_log for critical automation
rules.rationale: Any automated job that touches supervisor alerts, compliance records, or financial data (reorder costs) must write to `automation_log`.

rules.section: Solo Delete Must Issue a DB DELETE — In-Memory Filter Is Not Enough
rules.summary: Solo delete must call db.delete() explicitly
rules.rationale: In-memory cache filtering is a side effect of a successful DB delete — not a substitute for it.

rules.section: Sub-Queries via `.in('child_id', ids)` Must Add `.eq('hive_id', HIVE_ID)` Explicitly
rules.summary: Sub-queries must add hive_id scope explicitly
rules.rationale: The child table has its own hive_id column and must be filtered independently.

rules.section: pgvector UNION ALL — Each SELECT Must Be a Subquery
rules.summary: pgvector UNION ALL must use subquery pattern
rules.rationale: Every pgvector multi-source semantic search query must use the subquery pattern.

rules.summary: Python statistical functions must guard zero variance
rules.rationale: Before calling scipy.stats.spearmanr, pearsonr, or linregress, always verify the input series has non-zero standard deviation.

rules.section: Server-Side Search with ilike via .or() — Escape User Input
rules.summary: Server-side search must escape user input

rules.pattern: SELECT .*? FROM \(.*?\) .*? UNION ALL .*? SELECT .*? FROM \(.*?\) .*?
rules.rationale: ORDER BY and LIMIT cannot appear on individual members of a UNION ALL

rules.section: Python Calc Handlers — Return Keys Must Match Renderer Field Names Exactly
rules.summary: Ensure Python returned keys match renderer field names exactly
rules.rationale: Python returning different names causes every dependent display to show undefined

rules.section: AbortSignal.timeout is required on ALL Groq fetch() calls
rules.summary: All fetch() calls to api.groq.com must include a signal with AbortSignal.timeout
rules.rationale: Every fetch() to api.groq.com must include a timeout; without it, a slow or rate‑limited Groq response hangs the Supabase Edge Function.

rules.section: AbortSignal.timeout is required on ALL Groq fetch() calls
rules.summary: fetch() calls to api.groq.com must not omit AbortSignal.timeout
rules.rationale: Every fetch() to api.groq.com must include a timeout; missing it blocks the worker request.

rules.section: Embed functions are fire-and-forget by design — never add await
rules.summary: Calls to embedFaultEntry, embedSkillEntry, embedPMEntry must not be prefixed by await
rules.rationale: embed functions run in the background; adding await can freeze the save operation on Groq timeouts.

rules.section: Voice transcript must be length-capped before passing to callAI
rules.summary: Transcripts must be trimmed and sliced to 500 characters before callAI
rules.rationale: Long transcripts can exhaust TPM budget or enable prompt injection; they must be capped at 500 characters.

rules.section: Voice transcript must be length-capped before passing to callAI
rules.summary: callAI should be invoked with the capped safeTranscript variable
rules.rationale: Ensures the length‑capped transcript is used when calling the LLM edge function.

rules.summary: Design KPI definitions before building any dashboard
rules.pattern: Design KPI definitions before building any dashboard
rules.rationale: Designing KPI definitions before building any dashboard ensures that the metrics are meaningful and actionable.

rules.summary: Build Stage 3 manager dashboards using Recharts or Tremor
rules.pattern: Build Stage 3 manager dashboards using Recharts or Tremor
rules.rationale: Building Stage 3 manager dashboards using Recharts or Tremor ensures that the dashboards are visually appealing and easy to understand.

rules.summary: Create cross-hive analytics queries
rules.rationale: Creating cross-hive analytics queries enables managers to compare the performance of multiple teams.

rules.summary: Design Metabase or Grafana reporting for internal operations monitoring
rules.pattern: Design Metabase or Grafana reporting for internal operations monitoring
rules.rationale: Designing Metabase or Grafana reporting for internal operations monitoring enables managers to monitor the performance of internal operations.

rules.summary: Define the data model and queries needed to power each metric
rules.pattern: Define the data model and queries needed to power each metric
rules.rationale: Defining the data model and queries needed to power each metric ensures that the metrics are accurate and reliable.

rules.summary: Ensure all analytics respect hive data isolation
rules.pattern: Ensure all analytics respect hive data isolation
rules.rationale: Ensuring all analytics respect hive data isolation ensures that managers can only see the data relevant to their hive.

rules.summary: Never put 6 metrics on one chart; each visualization answers one question
rules.pattern: Never put 6 metrics on one chart; each visualization answers one question
rules.rationale: Never putting 6 metrics on one chart ensures that each visualization is clear and easy to understand.

rules.summary: Managers also check dashboards on phone; tables over complex charts on mobile
rules.pattern: Managers also check dashboards on phone; tables over complex charts on mobile
rules.rationale: Designing mobile-readable dashboards ensures that managers can access the data they need on-the-go.

rules.summary: Any analytics query over 500ms needs an index or a materialized view
rules.pattern: Any analytics query over 500ms needs an index or a materialized view
rules.rationale: Optimizing slow queries ensures that dashboards load quickly and efficiently.

rules.summary: Current data: logbook entries, checklist completions, parts usage, dayplanner schedules
rules.pattern: Current data: logbook entries, checklist completions, parts usage, dayplanner schedules
rules.rationale: Understanding the current data sources ensures that analytics are accurate and reliable.

rules.summary: Current analytics: None — this is entirely future work
rules.pattern: Current analytics: None — this is entirely future work
rules.rationale: Understanding the current analytics landscape ensures that future analytics efforts are aligned with business goals.

rules.summary: Stage 2 analytics: Per-hive health (fault frequency, backlog, top recurring faults)
rules.pattern: Stage 2 analytics: Per-hive health (fault frequency, backlog, top recurring faults)
rules.rationale: Implementing Stage 2 analytics ensures that managers have visibility into per-hive health metrics.

rules.summary: Stage 3 analytics: Cross-hive OEE, planned vs reactive ratio, workforce performance, maintenance cost
rules.pattern: Stage 3 analytics: Cross-hive OEE, planned vs reactive ratio, workforce performance, maintenance cost
rules.rationale: Implementing Stage 3 analytics ensures that managers have visibility into cross-hive metrics.

rules.summary: Stack: Recharts or Tremor for in-app charts; Metabase for internal ops; Grafana for infrastructure
rules.pattern: Stack: Recharts or Tremor for in-app charts; Metabase for internal ops; Grafana for infrastructure
rules.rationale: Using Recharts or Tremor for in-app charts ensures that dashboards are visually appealing and easy to understand.

rules.summary: All date range filters must use indexed columns (`created_at`, `date`)
rules.pattern: All date range filters must use indexed columns (`created_at`, `date`)
rules.rationale: Using indexed columns for date range filters ensures that queries are efficient and fast.

rules.summary: Cross-hive queries must filter by `hive_id IN (...)` — never full table scans
rules.pattern: Cross-hive queries must filter by `hive_id IN (...)` — never full table scans
rules.rationale: Filtering cross-hive queries by `hive_id IN (...)` ensures that queries are efficient and fast.

rules.summary: Cache dashboard results for 5 minutes — analytics do not need to be real-time (use real-time for alerts instead)
rules.pattern: Cache dashboard results for 5 minutes — analytics do not need to be real-time (use real-time for alerts instead)
rules.rationale: Caching dashboard results ensures that dashboards load quickly and efficiently.

rules.section: Custom Composite Scores Must Be Labeled as Custom
rules.summary: Equipment health scores, risk scores, and any weighted composite KPI that is NOT from a published standard must be clearly labeled as "custom" or "inspired by"
rules.pattern: Equipment health scores, risk scores, and any weighted composite KPI that is NOT from a published standard must be clearly labeled as "custom" or "inspired by"
rules.rationale: Labeling custom composite scores ensures transparency and trust in analytics.

rules.section: RAG Dashboard Pattern — Red/Amber/Green Thresholds Must Be Documented
rules.summary: Document RAG thresholds for KPI cards
rules.pattern: Every KPI card with RAG coloring needs documented thresholds.
rules.rationale: Without documentation, thresholds are arbitrary and can't be audited or adjusted.

rules.section: Role-Based Dashboard Views Are Essential for Field Workers
rules.summary: Use role toggle for analytics features with > 5 KPIs
rules.pattern: Any analytics feature with more than 5 KPIs needs a role toggle.
rules.rationale: A single analytics dashboard serving field technicians, supervisors, and managers creates information overload for all three.

rules.summary: Include KPI definition in analytics output
rules.pattern: 1. **KPI definition** — name, formula, unit, thresholds for good/warning/critical
rules.rationale: Provide clear information about KPIs for users.

rules.section: Statistical Correlation — Zero-Variance Guard Required Before scipy Calls
rules.summary: Add zero-variance guard for correlation functions
rules.pattern: Any analytics function that computes correlation must check for zero variance before calling `spearmanr`, `pearsonr`, or `linregress`.
rules.rationale: A group where all workers share the same value produces a constant array — `spearmanr` throws a division-by-zero exception.

rules.section: PM Compliance — due_count Must Scale With Period Length
rules.summary: Scale PM compliance denominator with period length
rules.pattern: PM compliance denominator must always be `period_days / freq_days`, not a flat 1.
rules.rationale: This makes a 90-day report show identical compliance percentages as a 30-day report for the same monthly task.

rules.section: Plotly Charts — Tab Cache Pattern for Analytics Dashboards
rules.summary: Cache Plotly chart data and check cache before fetching
rules.pattern: When an analytics dashboard loads 4 phases in parallel and the user can switch tabs instantly, each phase's render function must cache its data and check the cache before fetching.
rules.rationale: Plotly `newPlot` should only be called once per dataset — not on every tab switch.

rules.section: Prescriptive Analytics — "All Green" Is a Data Problem, Not a Success
rules.summary: Review health score formulas for missing data handling
rules.pattern: Any health score or risk score that defaults to the maximum value on missing data must be reviewed.
rules.rationale: Missing data is not safety — it is a blind spot.

skipped.rationale: Provide clear information about KPIs for users.

rules.section: DB Write Must Confirm Before localStorage Write
rules.summary: DB write must confirm before localStorage write
rules.rationale: Prevents phantom supervisor roles, ghost hive memberships, or duplicate records on the next load.

rules.section: Fire-and-Forget Supabase Writes Must Surface Errors
rules.summary: Supabase write must surface errors
rules.rationale: Ensures user-visible state is not silently lost due to errors.

rules.section: Hive Membership Validation — Scope Covers All Data-Loading Pages
rules.summary: Validate hive membership before loading team data
rules.rationale: Prevents removed workers from seeing team inventory, team assets, or team logs.

rules.section: localStorage Is Not a Source of Truth for Membership
rules.summary: localStorage is a cache, not a store
rules.rationale: Ensures user-owned state is stored in Supabase, not just localStorage.

rules.section: localStorage Is Not a Source of Truth for Membership
rules.summary: Recover from Supabase when localStorage is empty
rules.rationale: Ensures user-owned state can be recovered from Supabase on every page load.

rules.summary: Shared catalog tables must include governance columns
rules.rationale: Prevents fragmentation by ensuring all shared data flows through an approval layer

rules.summary: Hive gate must block page rendering when no hive ID exists
rules.rationale: Prevents solo data from being saved with no hive_id, which breaks team queries

rules.summary: Hive membership must be re-validated against DB on load
rules.rationale: Prevents stale localStorage from allowing access after membership revocation

rules.summary: HIVE_ID and HIVE_ROLE must be declared as let
rules.rationale: let declarations allow validation function to correct stale values

rules.section: Role-Based UI Enforcement at Load Time
rules.summary: Privileged controls must be hidden synchronously at load
rules.rationale: Prevents UI flicker and unauthorized access during async checks

rules.section: Shared Utils — escHtml, debounce, and Common Utilities
rules.summary: escHtml must escape single quotes to prevent XSS
rules.rationale: The `'` → `'` replacement is the most commonly missing escape, required for inline event handler safety

rules.section: Shared Utils — escHtml, debounce, and Common Utilities
rules.summary: debounce function must match the canonical implementation
rules.rationale: Canonical debounce is required to avoid duplicated timers across pages

rules.section: Draft Auto-Save Pattern for Field Forms
rules.summary: Draft auto‑save key must include the worker name to avoid cross‑user bleed
rules.rationale: Draft keys are worker‑scoped so shared devices don’t leak drafts between users

rules.section: Draft Auto-Save Pattern for Field Forms
rules.summary: Draft listener should be attached to the <form> element, not individual fields
rules.rationale: A single listener on the form ensures every field change triggers a draft save

rules.section: Transient UI State Must Be Persisted to Be Editable
rules.summary: Picker/selector values must be stored in a JSONB column before addEntry
rules.rationale: Persisting picker values lets the edit modal display and modify prior selections

rules.section: Dashboard / Welcome Stats Must Reflect Live Tables
rules.summary: Retired tables must not be queried in dashboard or welcome stats
rules.rationale: Counts from retired tables return stale or zero data, misleading users

rules.section: Shared Utils — escHtml, debounce, and Common Utilities
rules.summary: utils.js must be loaded after the Supabase CDN script and before any inline page script
rules.rationale: Loading utils.js after Supabase ensures the client is available, and before inline scripts lets pages use shared utilities

rules.section: Feature Config Maps Must Cover All Reachable Form Values
rules.summary: Update lookup maps when adding new form options
rules.pattern: lookup map must include both form-submitted value AND DB-stored canonical value as keys
rules.rationale: Silent feature breaks when new form options are added without updating the map

rules.section: New Page Deployment — Two Registries Must Be Updated Together
rules.summary: Update nav-hub and index.html stageData in the same commit
rules.pattern: nav-hub entry included in the same PR as the page itself
rules.rationale: Invisible pages to new users when registries are not updated together

rules.section: New Page Spec Must Name the Exact Identity Key
rules.summary: Specify exact localStorage key for worker identity
rules.pattern: exact localStorage key to read — not leave it to Frontend to guess
rules.rationale: Silent redirects with no error when wrong key is used

rules.section: WorkerFilter Pattern — Only Apply to Tables That Have the Column
rules.summary: Only apply workerFilter to tables with both hive_id and worker_name columns
rules.pattern: table has both hive_id and worker_name columns
rules.rationale: Silent Supabase errors when applying workerFilter to tables without required columns

rules.section: Schema design rule: Child tables accessed only via parent FK (e.g., asset_id) do not need worker_name — use .in('parent_id', parentIds) instead
rules.summary: Document access pattern in schema comment for child tables
rules.pattern: document access pattern in schema comment
rules.rationale: Future queries don't guess access pattern for child tables

rules.section: Foreign Key Column Type Must Match Exactly — uuid vs text
rules.summary: FK column type must match referenced column type
rules.rationale: Supabase will reject the constraint with error 42804: foreign key constraint cannot be implemented — incompatible types.

rules.summary: Bidirectional linking does NOT require a FK in both directions
rules.rationale: Bidirectional linking does NOT require a FK in both directions. Decide the "owner" of each relationship.

rules.section: Prevent Duplicates at Entry, Not at Display
rules.summary: Block insert if existing record with same natural key exists
rules.rationale: Before inserting a new record, query for an existing record with the same natural key (name, tag ID, serial). If found, block the insert and redirect to the existing record.

rules.section: Form Category vs. Asset-Specific Data — Decouple Them
rules.summary: Form fields that classify the job must NOT be used to filter asset-specific data
rules.rationale: Form fields that classify the job (e.g., category = Mechanical / Electrical) must NOT be used to filter asset-specific data (e.g., PM tasks).

rules.section: Smart Collapse for Long Lists — Pre-Compute Status at Fetch Time
rules.summary: Compute status at fetch/load time and attach it to each item object
rules.rationale: When a list has items of varying urgency (overdue, due soon, on track), compute status at fetch/load time and attach it to each item object.

rules.summary: Use Layer 1 or 2 first for free and minimal infrastructure
rules.rationale: Reach for Layer 1 or 2 first — they are already inside Supabase, require no additional infrastructure, and are free.

rules.summary: Keep SQL minimal; keep logic in Edge Functions
rules.pattern: pg_cron jobs call Edge Functions via HTTP — the Edge Function contains all the logic
rules.rationale: pg_cron jobs call Edge Functions via HTTP — the Edge Function contains all the logic. The SQL is just the trigger.

rules.section: DB Webhook — Instant Reaction to Data Changes
rules.summary: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget
rules.pattern: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget
rules.rationale: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget. If the Edge Function fails, there is no retry by default.

rules.summary: Use n8n for time delays and external service integrations
rules.pattern: n8n is the right tool only when a workflow needs: \(a\) time delays between steps or \(b\) external service integrations
rules.rationale: n8n is the right tool only when a workflow needs: (a) time delays between steps or (b) external service integrations not worth building a custom Edge Function for.

rules.summary: Resend is the email provider of choice
rules.pattern: Resend is the email provider of choice
rules.rationale: Resend is the email provider of choice. It has the most generous free tier, clean API, and a Supabase Edge Function integration in one HTTP call.

rules.summary: Store inputs, results, and narrative separately in JSONB
rules.pattern: Store inputs, results, AND narrative separately in JSONB
rules.rationale: Store inputs, results, AND narrative separately in JSONB. This allows re-rendering the report from history without re-calling the AI.

rules.summary: Keep 4 places in sync when adding a new calc type
rules.rationale: Ensure consistency across all 4 places when adding a new calc type

rules.section: New Discipline Adds 5 More Sync Points (Total 9)
rules.summary: List all 9 sync points when adding a new discipline
rules.rationale: Prevent silent undefined results or 400/500 errors when adding a new discipline

rules.section: Mode-Dependent Hidden Sections Must Not Submit Stale Data
rules.summary: Exclude hidden fields from submitted payload
rules.rationale: Prevent silent corruption of calculations due to stale values

rules.section: Mandatory Minimums Are Edge Function Constants, Not Client Defaults
rules.summary: Enforce minimum values in Edge Function, not form defaults
rules.rationale: Prevent silent under-sizing of safety-critical systems

rules.section: Feature Support Maps — Key by All Relevant Dimensions, Not Just One
rules.summary: Key support maps by all relevant dimensions
rules.rationale: Prevent collisions and restructure costs when adding new categories

rules.section: Hive Gate Scope — Only Gate Pages With Hive-Scoped Data
rules.summary: Only gate pages with hive-scoped data
rules.rationale: Prevent silent blocking of solo users

rules.section: Flow Check Formulas — Verify the Pass/Fail Ratio Is Not Constant
rules.summary: Design flow ratio should not be constant
rules.rationale: Using average operating hours instead of peak hours for design flow

rules.section: Flow Check Formulas — Verify the Pass/Fail Ratio Is Not Constant
rules.summary: Design flow ratio should vary with user input
rules.rationale: Using peak operating hours for design flow

rules.section: Engineering Calc — Impedance Must Use Complex Magnitude, Not Scalar Sum
rules.summary: Impedance calculation should use complex magnitude
rules.rationale: Ignoring cable reactance in impedance calculation

rules.section: Engineering Calc — Location-Keyed Lookup Tables for Per-City Defaults
rules.summary: Use location-keyed lookup tables for per-city defaults
rules.rationale: Scattering city-specific engineering constants in form defaults

rules.section: Audit Log Writes Must Be Fire-and-Forget
rules.summary: Audit log writes should be fire-and-forget
rules.rationale: Blocking audit log inserts

rules.section: Query-First Architecture for Team/Multi-User Data Views
rules.summary: Use query-first architecture for team/multi-user data views
rules.pattern: searchTeam\(append\): server query + keyset page variable\(\_teamLastDate\)
rules.rationale: Auto-loading data for team views

rules.section: Edit-in-Place vs Parallel Modal — Decision Rule
rules.summary: Use edit-in-place for complex forms
rules.pattern: form has more than 5 fields|form has conditional sections|form has rich input
rules.rationale: Prevents silent failures and data drift

rules.summary: Update four files in the same commit when adding a new Edge Function
rules.rationale: Ensures consistent configuration and prevents silent failures

rules.summary: Platform fee must be consistent across files
rules.pattern: PLATFORM_FEE_PCT = 0.05 in marketplace-checkout AND PLATFORM_FEE_PCT = 0.05 in marketplace-release
rules.rationale: Ensures consistent configuration and prevents silent failures

rules.summary: Migration timestamp must be unique
rules.rationale: Prevents silent failures and data drift

rules.summary: Order status state machine must be consistent
rules.pattern: 6 states: pending_payment → escrow_hold → released / refunded / disputed in marketplace_orders.status CHECK constraint + UI labels
rules.rationale: Ensures consistent configuration and prevents silent failures

rules.summary: Migration must include ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE for new tables.
rules.rationale: Skipping this is a silent failure — the listener compiles, the page works, but nothing fans out to other clients.

rules.section: Hive-scope filters in helper functions — parameterise, don't hardcode
rules.summary: Helper functions should not hardcode HIVE_ID in hive_id filter.
rules.rationale: Hardcoding HIVE_ID blocks cross-hive features and prevents future reuse.

rules.summary: Every SELECT on a table with deleted_at must filter deleted_at IS NULL.
rules.rationale: Missed sites leak deleted rows into views that should hide them.

rules.section: Dashboard / Welcome Stats Must Reflect Live Tables
rules.summary: Retired tables must not be queried in dashboard or welcome stats
rules.rationale: Counts from retired tables return stale or zero data, misleading users

rules.section: Shared Utils — escHtml, debounce, and Common Utilities
rules.summary: utils.js must be loaded after the Supabase CDN script and before any inline page script
rules.rationale: Loading utils.js after Supabase ensures the client is available, and before inline scripts lets pages use shared utilities

rules.section: Feature Config Maps Must Cover All Reachable Form Values
rules.summary: Update lookup maps when adding new form options
rules.pattern: lookup map must include both form-submitted value AND DB-stored canonical value as keys
rules.rationale: Silent feature breaks when new form options are added without updating the map

rules.section: New Page Deployment — Two Registries Must Be Updated Together
rules.summary: Update nav-hub and index.html stageData in the same commit
rules.pattern: nav-hub entry included in the same PR as the page itself
rules.rationale: Invisible pages to new users when registries are not updated together

rules.section: New Page Spec Must Name the Exact Identity Key
rules.summary: Specify exact localStorage key for worker identity
rules.pattern: exact localStorage key to read — not leave it to Frontend to guess
rules.rationale: Silent redirects with no error when wrong key is used

rules.section: WorkerFilter Pattern — Only Apply to Tables That Have the Column
rules.summary: Only apply workerFilter to tables with both hive_id and worker_name columns
rules.pattern: table has both hive_id and worker_name columns
rules.rationale: Silent Supabase errors when applying workerFilter to tables without required columns

rules.section: Schema design rule: Child tables accessed only via parent FK (e.g., asset_id) do not need worker_name — use .in('parent_id', parentIds) instead
rules.summary: Document access pattern in schema comment for child tables
rules.pattern: document access pattern in schema comment
rules.rationale: Future queries don't guess access pattern for child tables

rules.section: Foreign Key Column Type Must Match Exactly — uuid vs text
rules.summary: FK column type must match referenced column type
rules.rationale: Supabase will reject the constraint with error 42804: foreign key constraint cannot be implemented — incompatible types.

rules.summary: Bidirectional linking does NOT require a FK in both directions
rules.rationale: Bidirectional linking does NOT require a FK in both directions. Decide the "owner" of each relationship.

rules.section: Prevent Duplicates at Entry, Not at Display
rules.summary: Block insert if existing record with same natural key exists
rules.rationale: Before inserting a new record, query for an existing record with the same natural key (name, tag ID, serial). If found, block the insert and redirect to the existing record.

rules.section: Form Category vs. Asset-Specific Data — Decouple Them
rules.summary: Form fields that classify the job must NOT be used to filter asset-specific data
rules.rationale: Form fields that classify the job (e.g., category = Mechanical / Electrical) must NOT be used to filter asset-specific data (e.g., PM tasks).

rules.section: Smart Collapse for Long Lists — Pre-Compute Status at Fetch Time
rules.summary: Compute status at fetch/load time and attach it to each item object
rules.rationale: When a list has items of varying urgency (overdue, due soon, on track), compute status at fetch/load time and attach it to each item object.

rules.summary: Use Layer 1 or 2 first for free and minimal infrastructure
rules.rationale: Reach for Layer 1 or 2 first — they are already inside Supabase, require no additional infrastructure, and are free.

rules.summary: Keep SQL minimal; keep logic in Edge Functions
rules.pattern: pg_cron jobs call Edge Functions via HTTP — the Edge Function contains all the logic
rules.rationale: pg_cron jobs call Edge Functions via HTTP — the Edge Function contains all the logic. The SQL is just the trigger.

rules.section: DB Webhook — Instant Reaction to Data Changes
rules.summary: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget
rules.pattern: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget
rules.rationale: DB Webhooks are stateless — they fire and forget. If the Edge Function fails, there is no retry by default.

rules.summary: Use n8n for time delays and external service integrations
rules.pattern: n8n is the right tool only when a workflow needs: \(a\) time delays between steps or \(b\) external service integrations
rules.rationale: n8n is the right tool only when a workflow needs: (a) time delays between steps or (b) external service integrations not worth building a custom Edge Function for.

rules.summary: Resend is the email provider of choice
rules.pattern: Resend is the email provider of choice
rules.rationale: Resend is the email provider of choice. It has the most generous free tier, clean API, and a Supabase Edge Function integration in one HTTP call.

rules.summary: Store inputs, results, and narrative separately in JSONB
rules.pattern: Store inputs, results, AND narrative separately in JSONB
rules.rationale: Store inputs, results, AND narrative separately in JSONB. This allows re-rendering the report from history without re-calling the AI.

rules.summary: Keep 4 places in sync when adding a new calc type
rules.rationale: Ensure consistency across all 4 places when adding a new calc type

rules.section: New Discipline Adds 5 More Sync Points (Total 9)
rules.summary: List all 9 sync points when adding a new discipline
rules.rationale: Prevent silent undefined results or 400/500 errors when adding a new discipline

rules.section: Mode-Dependent Hidden Sections Must Not Submit Stale Data
rules.summary: Exclude hidden fields from submitted payload
rules.rationale: Prevent silent corruption of calculations due to stale values

rules.section: Mandatory Minimums Are Edge Function Constants, Not Client Defaults
rules.summary: Enforce minimum values in Edge Function, not form defaults
rules.rationale: Prevent silent under-sizing of safety-critical systems

rules.section: Feature Support Maps — Key by All Relevant Dimensions, Not Just One
rules.summary: Key support maps by all relevant dimensions
rules.rationale: Prevent collisions and restructure costs when adding new categories

rules.section: Hive Gate Scope — Only Gate Pages With Hive-Scoped Data
rules.summary: Only gate pages with hive-scoped data
rules.rationale: Prevent silent blocking of solo users

rules.section: Flow Check Formulas — Verify the Pass/Fail Ratio Is Not Constant
rules.summary: Design flow ratio should not be constant
rules.rationale: Using average operating hours instead of peak hours for design flow

rules.section: Flow Check Formulas — Verify the Pass/Fail Ratio Is Not Constant
rules.summary: Design flow ratio should vary with user input
rules.rationale: Using peak operating hours for design flow

rules.section: Engineering Calc — Impedance Must Use Complex Magnitude, Not Scalar Sum
rules.summary: Impedance calculation should use complex magnitude
rules.rationale: Ignoring cable reactance in impedance calculation

rules.section: Engineering Calc — Location-Keyed Lookup Tables for Per-City Defaults
rules.summary: Use location-keyed lookup tables for per-city defaults
rules.rationale: Scattering city-specific engineering constants in form defaults

rules.section: Audit Log Writes Must Be Fire-and-Forget
rules.summary: Audit log writes should be fire-and-forget
rules.rationale: Blocking audit log inserts

rules.section: Query-First Architecture for Team/Multi-User Data Views
rules.summary: Use query-first architecture for team/multi-user data views
rules.pattern: searchTeam\(append\): server query + keyset page variable\(\_teamLastDate\)
rules.rationale: Auto-loading data for team views

rules.section: Edit-in-Place vs Parallel Modal — Decision Rule
rules.summary: Use edit-in-place for complex forms
rules.pattern: form has more than 5 fields|form has conditional sections|form has rich input
rules.rationale: Prevents silent failures and data drift

rules.summary: Update four files in the same commit when adding a new Edge Function
rules.rationale: Ensures consistent configuration and prevents silent failures

rules.summary: Platform fee must be consistent across files
rules.pattern: PLATFORM_FEE_PCT = 0.05 in marketplace-checkout AND PLATFORM_FEE_PCT = 0.05 in marketplace-release
rules.rationale: Ensures consistent configuration and prevents silent failures

rules.summary: Migration timestamp must be unique
rules.rationale: Prevents silent failures and data drift

rules.summary: Order status state machine must be consistent
rules.pattern: 6 states: pending_payment → escrow_hold → released / refunded / disputed in marketplace_orders.status CHECK constraint + UI labels
rules.rationale: Ensures consistent configuration and prevents silent failures

rules.summary: Migration must include ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE for new tables.
rules.rationale: Skipping this is a silent failure — the listener compiles, the page works, but nothing fans out to other clients.

rules.section: Hive-scope filters in helper functions — parameterise, don't hardcode
rules.summary: Helper functions should not hardcode HIVE_ID in hive_id filter.
rules.rationale: Hardcoding HIVE_ID blocks cross-hive features and prevents future reuse.

rules.summary: Every SELECT on a table with deleted_at must filter deleted_at IS NULL.
rules.rationale: Missed sites leak deleted rows into views that should hide them.

rules.summary: New edge functions require a config.toml entry

rules.summary: Resend requires domain verification before using a custom from address
rules.rationale: Emails sent via Resend from `anything@yourdomain.com` will fail until the domain is verified in the Resend dashboard.

rules.summary: Service worker caches old HTML after deploy

rules.rationale: Check for sensitive data — never deploy `.env` files or credentials

rules.summary: Deploy script must cover every edge function
rules.rationale: `deploy-functions.ps1` must list every function in `supabase/functions/*/`.

rules.summary: Use dynamic CORS headers
rules.rationale: All edge functions must import and call `getCorsHeaders(req)` from `_shared/cors.ts`.

rules.section: deploy-functions.ps1 Must Cover Every Edge Function

rules.rationale: `deploy-functions.ps1` must list every function in `supabase/functions/*/`.

rules.section: Supabase Edge Functions — Separate Deploy Step
rules.summary: Deploy Edge Functions separately from frontend
rules.rationale: Missing step 2 means the frontend is updated but the backend still runs old code — a silent mismatch that is hard to debug.

rules.section: Supabase Edge Functions — Separate Deploy Step
rules.summary: Deploy Edge Functions with --no-verify-jwt
rules.rationale: **`--no-verify-jwt` is MANDATORY and is RESET on every deploy.**

rules.section: Supabase Edge Functions — Separate Deploy Step
rules.summary: Use signed JWT for Edge Functions
rules.rationale: **`sb_publishable_` key format does NOT satisfy `verify_jwt = true`.**

rules.section: Deploy checklist for Edge Function changes
rules.summary: Run deploy checklist for Edge Function changes
rules.rationale: **Deploy checklist for Edge Function changes:**

rules.summary: Add new secrets via Supabase Dashboard
rules.pattern: Add via Supabase Dashboard: Edge Functions > Secrets
rules.rationale: **Adding new secrets checklist:**

rules.section: Checklist when rotating or adding a secret
rules.summary: Rotate secrets by redeploying Edge Functions
rules.rationale: **Checklist when rotating or adding a secret:**

rules.section: Netlify CDN Cache — Users Need Hard Refresh After Deploy
rules.summary: Perform hard refresh after deploy
rules.pattern: Ctrl + Shift + R|Cmd + Shift + R|F12 → right-click the refresh button → "Empty cache and hard reload"
rules.rationale: **When this matters:** Any time a user reports "I don't see the changes" after a confirmed deploy.

rules.summary: Set PowerShell execution policy to RemoteSigned
rules.rationale: On Windows, `npx` commands fail with "running scripts is disabled on this system" until the execution policy is set.

rules.section: Diagnosing Edge Function 500 Errors — curl Is the Only Reliable Method
rules.summary: Diagnose Edge Function 500 errors with curl, not SDK
rules.rationale: The Supabase JS SDK hides the actual error body and only surfaces a non-2xx status code.

rules.section: API Validation After Every Edge Function Deploy
rules.summary: Validate API after every Edge Function deploy
rules.pattern: curl -s -X POST \"https://YOUR_PROJECT\.supabase\.co/functions/v1/.*\" -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANON_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{.*}' | python -m json\.tool
rules.rationale: The browser masks the real response body; curl exposes it.

rules.section: Context Compaction Resume — Edge Function May Be Committed But Not Deployed
rules.summary: Check Supabase dashboard deployment timestamp after session resume
rules.rationale: The frontend will show the new UI, but the edge function still runs the old code.

rules.section: Context Compaction Resume — Edge Function May Be Committed But Not Deployed
rules.summary: Deploy Edge Function after commit
rules.rationale: The frontend will show the new UI, but the edge function still runs the old code.

rules.summary: Hard refresh after propagation delay
rules.rationale: The browser may still hit 422 for 30–60 seconds while Supabase propagates the new code to all edge nodes.

rules.section: ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED on Supabase Edge Functions — DNS Hiccup, Not Code
rules.summary: Don't debug Edge Function for DNS error
rules.rationale: The function is unreachable, not broken.

rules.section: Large File Patching — Write-then-run Python Script
rules.summary: Use Python script for large file patching
rules.rationale: The Write tool is unaffected by VSCode's formatter.

rules.section: pg_cron Migrations Must Be Commented Out by Default
rules.summary: Wrap pg_cron in a block comment
rules.pattern: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_logbook_hive_date ON logbook \(hive_id, created_at DESC\);
rules.rationale: pg_cron requires the extension to be enabled first in Supabase Dashboard > Database > Extensions

rules.section: Render.com Free Tier — 2-3 Minute Deploy Delay After Push
rules.summary: Validate deployment before user testing
rules.rationale: Submitting a calc immediately after pushing hits the OLD code, giving misleading test results

rules.section: Netlify Deploy — Hard Refresh Required After HTML Changes
rules.summary: Instruct hard refresh after deploy
rules.rationale: The user's browser may still serve the old file from cache

rules.section: HTML Files in Active Development — Commit Overwrites Are Silent
rules.summary: Verify fix is still in the file after push
rules.rationale: A later commit can silently overwrite a fix from an earlier commit

rules.summary: Install exact apt packages for weasyprint
rules.pattern: libpango-1.0-0 \| libpangocairo-1.0-0 \| libpangoft2-1.0-0 \| libcairo2 \| libgdk-pixbuf-2.0-0 \| libharfbuzz0b \| libfontconfig1 \| fontconfig \| shared-mime-info \| fonts-liberation
rules.rationale: WeasyPrint on `python:3.11-slim` (Debian Bookworm) needs these exact apt packages

rules.summary: Exclude __pycache__ and .pyc files from git
rules.rationale: __pycache__ and .pyc files must be in .gitignore

rules.rationale: Ensures new Supabase Edge Functions are validated for CORS, error contracts, and input validation

rules.summary: fetch calls to api.groq.com must include AbortSignal.timeout
rules.rationale: Supabase kills the function after 150 seconds without a timeout, blocking the request

rules.summary: HTML pages must not contain file:// URLs
rules.rationale: Opening pages via file:// sends Origin: null, causing 401 Unauthorized from Supabase

rules.summary: Migrations adding realtime listeners must include ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE
rules.rationale: Listeners compile but no fan‑out occurs until the table is added to the supabase_realtime publication

rules.summary: Edge functions that call Groq must be listed in ALL_GROQ_FUNCTIONS in validate_groq_fallback.py
rules.rationale: Ensures Groq‑calling edge functions are covered by fallback validation logic

rules.rationale: Missing step 2 means the frontend is updated but the backend still runs old code — a silent mismatch that is hard to debug.

rules.section: Supabase Edge Functions — Separate Deploy Step
rules.summary: Deploy Edge Functions with --no-verify-jwt
rules.rationale: **`--no-verify-jwt` is MANDATORY and is RESET on every deploy.**

rules.section: Supabase Edge Functions — Separate Deploy Step
rules.summary: Use signed JWT for Edge Functions
rules.rationale: **`sb_publishable_` key format does NOT satisfy `verify_jwt = true`.**

rules.section: Deploy checklist for Edge Function changes
rules.summary: Run deploy checklist for Edge Function changes
rules.rationale: **Deploy checklist for Edge Function changes:**

rules.summary: Add new secrets via Supabase Dashboard
rules.pattern: Add via Supabase Dashboard: Edge Functions > Secrets
rules.rationale: **Adding new secrets checklist:**

rules.section: Checklist when rotating or adding a secret
rules.summary: Rotate secrets by redeploying Edge Functions
rules.rationale: **Checklist when rotating or adding a secret:**

rules.section: Netlify CDN Cache — Users Need Hard Refresh After Deploy
rules.summary: Perform hard refresh after deploy
rules.pattern: Ctrl + Shift + R|Cmd + Shift + R|F12 → right-click the refresh button → "Empty cache and hard reload"
rules.rationale: **When this matters:** Any time a user reports "I don't see the changes" after a confirmed deploy.

rules.summary: Set PowerShell execution policy to RemoteSigned
rules.rationale: On Windows, `npx` commands fail with "running scripts is disabled on this system" until the execution policy is set.

rules.section: Diagnosing Edge Function 500 Errors — curl Is the Only Reliable Method
rules.summary: Diagnose Edge Function 500 errors with curl, not SDK
rules.rationale: The Supabase JS SDK hides the actual error body and only surfaces a non-2xx status code.

rules.section: API Validation After Every Edge Function Deploy
rules.summary: Validate API after every Edge Function deploy
rules.pattern: curl -s -X POST \"https://YOUR_PROJECT\.supabase\.co/functions/v1/.*\" -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ANON_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{.*}' | python -m json\.tool
rules.rationale: The browser masks the real response body; curl exposes it.

rules.section: Context Compaction Resume — Edge Function May Be Committed But Not Deployed
rules.summary: Check Supabase dashboard deployment timestamp after session resume
rules.rationale: The frontend will show the new UI, but the edge function still runs the old code.

rules.section: Context Compaction Resume — Edge Function May Be Committed But Not Deployed
rules.summary: Deploy Edge Function after commit
rules.rationale: The frontend will show the new UI, but the edge function still runs the old code.

rules.summary: Hard refresh after propagation delay
rules.rationale: The browser may still hit 422 for 30–60 seconds while Supabase propagates the new code to all edge nodes.

rules.section: ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED on Supabase Edge Functions — DNS Hiccup, Not Code
rules.summary: Don't debug Edge Function for DNS error
rules.rationale: The function is unreachable, not broken.

rules.section: Large File Patching — Write-then-run Python Script
rules.summary: Use Python script for large file patching
rules.rationale: The Write tool is unaffected by VSCode's formatter.

rules.section: pg_cron Migrations Must Be Commented Out by Default
rules.summary: Wrap pg_cron in a block comment
rules.pattern: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_logbook_hive_date ON logbook \(hive_id, created_at DESC\);
rules.rationale: pg_cron requires the extension to be enabled first in Supabase Dashboard > Database > Extensions

rules.section: Render.com Free Tier — 2-3 Minute Deploy Delay After Push
rules.summary: Validate deployment before user testing
rules.rationale: Submitting a calc immediately after pushing hits the OLD code, giving misleading test results

rules.section: Netlify Deploy — Hard Refresh Required After HTML Changes
rules.summary: Instruct hard refresh after deploy
rules.rationale: The user's browser may still serve the old file from cache

rules.section: HTML Files in Active Development — Commit Overwrites Are Silent
rules.summary: Verify fix is still in the file after push
rules.rationale: A later commit can silently overwrite a fix from an earlier commit

rules.summary: Install exact apt packages for weasyprint
rules.pattern: libpango-1.0-0 \| libpangocairo-1.0-0 \| libpangoft2-1.0-0 \| libcairo2 \| libgdk-pixbuf-2.0-0 \| libharfbuzz0b \| libfontconfig1 \| fontconfig \| shared-mime-info \| fonts-liberation
rules.rationale: WeasyPrint on `python:3.11-slim` (Debian Bookworm) needs these exact apt packages

rules.summary: Exclude __pycache__ and .pyc files from git
rules.rationale: __pycache__ and .pyc files must be in .gitignore

rules.summary: Edge functions must be listed in ALL_FUNCTIONS in validate_edge_contracts.py
rules.rationale: Ensures new Supabase Edge Functions are validated for CORS, error contracts, and input validation

rules.summary: fetch calls to api.groq.com must include AbortSignal.timeout
rules.rationale: Supabase kills the function after 150 seconds without a timeout, blocking the request

rules.summary: HTML pages must not contain file:// URLs
rules.rationale: Opening pages via file:// sends Origin: null, causing 401 Unauthorized from Supabase

rules.summary: Migrations adding realtime listeners must include ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE
rules.rationale: Listeners compile but no fan‑out occurs until the table is added to the supabase_realtime publication

rules.summary: Edge functions that call Groq must be listed in ALL_GROQ_FUNCTIONS in validate_groq_fallback.py
rules.rationale: Ensures Groq‑calling edge functions are covered by fallback validation logic

rules.section: Hive Isolation Checklist — Run on Every New Feature
rules.summary: Supabase queries on hive-scoped data must include .eq('hive_id', HIVE_ID)
rules.rationale: Ensures hive isolation by scoping queries to the current hive

rules.section: Hive Isolation Checklist — Run on Every New Feature
rules.summary: Supabase queries on worker-scoped data must include .eq('worker_name', WORKER_NAME) or .eq('auth_uid', auth.uid())
rules.rationale: Ensures worker-level isolation

rules.section: Hive Isolation Checklist — Run on Every New Feature
rules.summary: .in('worker_name', ...) queries must also include .eq('hive_id', HIVE_ID)
rules.rationale: Prevents cross-hive data leakage when filtering by worker name

rules.section: Hive Isolation Checklist — Run on Every New Feature
rules.summary: After auth migration, .eq('worker_name', WORKER_NAME) is anti-pattern; use .eq('auth_uid', auth.uid())
rules.rationale: String-based membership checks are interim; use auth_uid for security

rules.section: Role-Gated UI Buttons — Template Level, Not Function Level
rules.summary: Supervisor-only buttons must not render for workers; avoid hidden buttons
rules.rationale: Hidden buttons can be unhidden and trigger actions

rules.section: Role-Gated UI Buttons — Template Level, Not Function Level
rules.summary: Supervisor-only button must be conditionally rendered, not hidden via JS
rules.rationale: Ensures UI does not expose actions to workers

rules.summary: Do not rely on localStorage for HIVE_ROLE; validate from DB
rules.rationale: localStorage can be tampered; must validate role from DB

rules.summary: Enable Cloudflare Turnstile in Supabase Auth dashboard
rules.rationale: Blocks automated account creation

rules.summary: Kicking a member sets status = 'kicked' on hive_members row
rules.rationale: Prevents kicked worker from rejoining via invite code flow

rules.section: Detail-View Hive Scope Must Match Picker Scope
rules.summary: Detail query uses same dual-query merge pattern as picker
rules.rationale: Prevents inventory items from disappearing between selection and detail steps

rules.section: Sub-Queries Fetched by Array Do Not Inherit Parent Hive Scope
rules.summary: Child query adds explicit hive_id filter
rules.rationale: Prevents child records from being fetched from any hive

rules.section: status=approved Required on Every Catalog Query Exposed to Users
rules.summary: Catalog query includes .eq('status', 'approved')
rules.rationale: Prevents pending items from being shown in UI

rules.summary: High-write tables have daily row limit per hive
rules.rationale: Prevents data flooding per hive

rules.section: L5 — Sub-Queries via `.in()` Must Add `.eq('hive_id', HIVE_ID)` Explicitly
rules.summary: Sub-queries via `.in()` must add `.eq('hive_id', HIVE_ID)` explicitly
rules.rationale: Without the explicit hive guard, a worker sees records from another hive.

rules.section: L6 — Edit Button Scope for Own Items Must Use `worker_name`, Not `status`
rules.summary: Edit button scope for own items must use `worker_name`, not `status`
rules.rationale: Status-based gating conflates ownership with approval state and produces two bugs simultaneously.

rules.section: L7 — Worker Edits of Approved Items Must Re-Enter Approval Queue
rules.summary: Worker edits of approved items must re-enter approval queue
rules.rationale: Never leave `editStatus = undefined` for approved items.

rules.section: L8 — Edit DB Update Must Scope by `worker_name` for Non-Supervisors
rules.summary: Edit DB update must scope by `worker_name` for non-supervisors
rules.rationale: The DB update must enforce this — not just the UI.

rules.summary: signOut() must clear all worker identity and hive context keys
rules.rationale: Incomplete signOut() can lead to cross-tenant contamination

rules.summary: Do not enable RLS on hive-scoped tables without Supabase Auth
rules.rationale: Premature RLS enablement can cause issues

rules.summary: Use JWT claims in RLS policies for efficient multi-tenancy
rules.rationale: Using JWT claims improves performance and scalability

rules.summary: Implement Supabase Auth before enabling RLS
rules.rationale: Supabase Auth is required for secure multi-tenancy

rules.summary: Clear hive context keys on signOut()
rules.rationale: Clearing hive context prevents cross-tenant contamination

rules.section: Branch-symmetry rule for hive-state localStorage writes
rules.summary: Write complete hive-state keys together
rules.rationale: Asymmetric writes cause recurring bugs; ensure complete set is written together

rules.section: Hive-scope filter — when required vs redundant
rules.summary: Scope list queries by hive_id
rules.rationale: Prevent cross-hive data leak; always scope list queries by hive_id

rules.section: Hive-scope filter — when required vs redundant
rules.summary: Avoid redundant hive_id filter on UUID-PK lookups
rules.rationale: Redundant filter can silently filter out cross-hive data; refactor helpers to take hive set as parameter

rules.section: Cross-hive realtime requires its own channel
rules.summary: Declare parallel channel for cross-hive features
rules.rationale: Ensure cross-hive features use a content filter in the subscription

rules.section: Audit log meta is extensible — pass domain context via `extraMeta`
rules.summary: Use writeAuditLog with extraMeta for domain context
rules.rationale: Keep audit log queries hive-scoped while providing richer context

rules.summary: Re-validate role from DB on load for role-gated pages
rules.rationale: Prevent role drift; re-validate role on load for role-gated pages
